macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Indexes Close Lower on March 26, Nasdaq Enters Correction
items.summary: The Dow fell 1.06%, S&P 500 lost 0.97%, Nasdaq dropped 1.31% entering correction territory, and Russell 2000 declined 1.15%, amid escalating Middle East tensions.

items.title: Trump Extends Iran Deadline by 10 Days, Oil Prices Climb
items.summary: President Trump extended his deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by 10 days, avoiding immediate air strikes, while oil prices rose due to ongoing conflict concerns; talks are reportedly progressing but disputed by Iran.

items.title: ECB's Lagarde Warns Equity Markets Too Optimistic on Iran Shock
items.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde cautioned that equity markets are overly optimistic amid the real shock from the Iranian conflict.

items.title: Asian and European Markets Close Sharply Lower on Middle East Fears
items.summary: Asian and European markets ended significantly down due to rising concerns over the escalating conflict in the Middle East involving Iran.

items.title: US Stock Futures Flat Ahead of Friday Open
items.summary: Stock futures are flat on March 27 following Thursday's declines, with markets set to open at 9:30 AM ET amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Drops $2,860 to $66,587 Amid Late-Week Fade
items.summary: Bitcoin fell to $66,587.39 at 9 a.m. ET on March 27, 2026, down from $69,438 yesterday and lower than a year ago. Kitco reports a late-week fade in April futures.

items.title: Ethereum Declines $84 to $1,988, Faces Early 2026 Volatility
items.summary: Ethereum traded at $1,988.69 at 9 a.m. ET on March 27, down $84 from yesterday amid sharp early 2026 declines due to recession fears and Vitalik Buterin sales. Long-term forecasts remain bullish, up to $40,000.

items.title: Bitcoin Nears Critical $68,000 Level Amid Iran Conflict Resilience
items.summary: One month into the Iran conflict, Bitcoin has outperformed other markets including gold, approaching $68,000 with potential turbulence from options expiry near $74,000. Positive weekly RSI and MACD signals persist.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Luma AI Launches Uni-1 Model Outscoring Google and OpenAI
items.summary: Luma AI released Uni-1, topping Google’s Nano Banana 2 and OpenAI’s GPT Image 1.5 on RISEBench with a score of 0.51 at 10-30% lower cost.

items.title: MIT Develops AI System Boosting Warehouse Robot Throughput by 25%
items.summary: MIT and Symbotic created a hybrid AI using deep reinforcement learning that increases warehouse robot efficiency by 25% via congestion-aware priority decisions.

items.title: Washington Governor Signs AI Safety and Transparency Bills
items.summary: Gov. Bob Ferguson signed two AI-related bills, including SB 1786 requiring provenance data for generative AI media.

items.title: AI Fluency Emerges as Driver of Workforce Inequality
items.summary: New research reveals experienced AI users outperform newcomers, widening productivity gaps and raising concerns for employment and economic opportunity.

items.title: China's Open-Source AI Momentum Threatens US Position
items.summary: A US advisory body warns China's dominance in open-source AI, fueled by low costs and industry integration, creates a self-reinforcing global advantage.

items.title: AI Research Shifts to World Models for Real-World Understanding
items.summary: Investment grows in world models simulating physical dynamics for robotics, with approaches like JEPA and generative spatial models addressing LLM limitations.

items.title: University of East London AI Transforms Personality Testing
items.summary: Machine learning achieves over 93% accuracy in DISC personality assessments and enables shorter questionnaires for faster, reliable results.

items.title: AI's Explosive Growth Sabotages Big Tech Climate Goals
items.summary: Rising natural gas use from AI data centers hinders Google and Microsoft's clean energy targets, shifting goals from moonshots to marathons.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Epic Games Lays Off Over 1,000 Employees
items.summary: Epic Games, parent of Fortnite, laid off more than 1,000 staff this week amid industry challenges. The cuts were described as a stark message from the video game giant.

items.title: Sony Shuts Down Dark Outlaw Games Studio
items.summary: PlayStation closed internal studio Dark Outlaw Games, led by former Call of Duty developer Jason Blundell, as a 'strategic adjustment.' This follows the shuttering of Bluepoint Games in February.

items.title: ByteDance Sells Moonton to Saudi's Savvy Games for $6 Billion
items.summary: Savvy Games Group, backed by Saudi sovereign wealth fund PIF, acquired mobile studio Moonton, known for Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, for $6 billion. This marks another major Saudi investment in gaming.

items.title: Crimson Desert Sells 3 Million Copies in Five Days
items.summary: Open-world RPG Crimson Desert from Pearl Abyss has sold over 3 million copies worldwide shortly after launch. The title has seen massive visibility on social media.

items.title: Meta Lays Off Staff in VR Division Reality Labs
items.summary: Meta conducted more layoffs in its VR-focused Reality Labs amid a shift toward AI, despite optimistic comments from its games director at GDC about new hardware and games.

items.title: Rocket League Championship Series Esports Event Draws Crowds
items.summary: A personal account highlights the growing mainstream appeal of esports at the Rocket League Championship Series tournament at Boston University's Agganis Arena.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Honda Scraps Electric Vehicle Collaboration with Sony
items.summary: Honda has canceled its joint electric vehicle project with Sony, scrapping the planned Afeela models amid shifting priorities in the EV sector.

items.title: Netflix Raises Prices Across All Streaming Tiers
items.summary: Netflix has increased prices on all plans, with the standard tier now at $19.99 and premium at $26.99, as part of ongoing adjustments in the streaming market.

items.title: Nintendo Raises Prices for Switch 2 Console
items.summary: Nintendo has increased the price of its upcoming Switch 2, reflecting changes in consumer preferences and market dynamics.

items.title: BRINC Launches Advanced Police Drone with Starlink
items.summary: BRINC introduced a new police drone equipped with Starlink connectivity, capable of 60 mph vehicle chases and carrying Narcan, called the most capable 911 response drone.

items.title: Simple CEO Alex Emelian Departs to Launch Stablerail
items.summary: Alex Emelian stepped down as CEO of crypto neobank Simple to start Stablerail, a bank-grade treasury platform for stablecoin payments.

items.title: Landmark Verdict in Social Media Harm Trial
items.summary: A court verdict on whether social media platforms deliberately harm young users could reshape the internet landscape and lead to thousands of similar cases.

items.title: Intel Core Ultra 270K and 250K Plus CPUs Reviewed
items.summary: Reviews highlight strong performance for Intel's new Core Ultra 270K and 250K Plus CPUs, though dependent on compatible RAM and SSDs.
